title: Detection of Sars-Cov2 Using Droplet Digital PCR
author: Joseph Patterson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be8tjhwn

steps.instruction: Add 11 µL of Master mix to each PCR tube used. •4.4 µL iScript Reverse Transcription Supermix for RT-qPCR (Bio-Rad 1708841). •6.6 µL DNase/RNase free water.
Add 11 µL of sample/diluted RNA to each tube (identify optimal dilution prior to performing the real experiment).
Close tubes and shake by hand to mix.
Centrifuge to collection samples at the bottom of the tubes.
Perform cDNA synthesis reaction in a Thermocycler (~30 min. total). •Constant lid temperature of 105ºC. •5 min. at 25ºC. •20 min. at 46ºC. •1 min. 95ºC • Hold at 4ºC.
Dispense 11 µL master mix to each PCR tube. •9 parts ddPCR Supermix for Probes (no dUTP) (Bio-Rad 186-3026). •1 part Probe
Transfer 11 µL of cDNA to each PCR tube containing master mix.
Mix samples and centrifuge to collect at the bottom of the tube.
Obtain a DG8 droplet generator cartridge (Bio-Rad 1864008) and transfer 20 µL of sample to the sample well.
Transfer 70 µL of droplet generation oil (Bio-Rad 1863005) to the oil well.
Secure the gasket (Bio-Rad 1863009) over the cartridge, and used the QX droplet generator (Bio-Rad 186-4002) to generate droplets.
Transfer 40 µL droplets to a 96 well plate (Bio-Rad 12001925).
When all droplets for the experiment have been transferred, cover plate with pierceable foil (Bio-Rad 181-4040) with the RED LINE SIDE UP AT THE TOP,
Heat seal with a plate sealer (Bio-Rad 181-4000).
Transfer plate to a Real-Time thermocycler. •10 min. at 95ºC •40 cycles (30 s 94ºC, 1 min. 60ºC) •10 min. at 98ºC. •Hold at 4ºC.
Transfer plate to droplet reader and run.
Analysis is performed with the Quantasoft software associated with the droplet reader.
Lower limit of quantitation is 10 copies/ul.
Lower limit of detection is 1 copy/ul.
A single present copy/ul in both N1 and N2 probes is classified as a suspected positive test.
A human RNaseP probe is used to confirm a quality sample of RNA was obtained from each subject.
A 500 copy/ul cDNA standard is run with the N1 and N2 probes on each plate as a positive control.
A human RNaseP, SARS-Cov2 N1, or SARS-Cov2 N2 probe is used in each reaction.
In addition cDNA made from human samples, a comercial cDNA control for SARS-Cov2 is also used with each probe as a control.
